key_events.summary: Fed Chair Powell signaled September rate cut, cited rising downside risks to employment. Removed 'shortfalls from maximum employment' wording — dovish structural shift. Market rallied: S&P 500 +1.5%, Nasdaq ~+2%. 10Y yields fell. CME FedWatch probability for Sep cut jumped from 75% to 87%+ on the day.
key_events.market_impact: Strongly bullish equities, bullish bonds (yields down), bearish USD

key_events.summary: NVDA reported revenue $46.7B (+56% YoY, +6% QoQ). EPS: GAAP $1.08 / Non-GAAP $1.05. Guidance Q3: $54B (+/-2%). Data center revenue beat overall but missed some street estimates for the second consecutive quarter. Stock initially slipped in AH trading, pared losses next day.
key_events.market_impact: Muted/negative for NVDA itself (data center miss again), neutral-positive for broader semis

key_events.summary: NFP: ~22,000 (vs est 75,000, catastrophic miss). Unemployment rose to 4.3%. ADP private payrolls earlier in week: 54,000 (vs est 75,000). CME FedWatch Sep cut probability: 99.3%. Market rallied on bad news (bad economy = more cuts). S&P 500 closed week solidly above 6,400.
key_events.market_impact: Bullish equities (bad = good for cuts), very bullish TLT/bonds, bearish USD, bullish gold

macro_context.regime_summary: Dovish pivot confirmed. The entire period from 8/20 to 9/5 was a rate-cut-euphoria regime. Labor market softening provided cover for Fed easing. Risk-on with rotation toward rate-sensitive assets (gold, bonds, dividend plays). Tech broadly flat-to-down post-NVDA earnings as whisper numbers vs. guidance disappointed even though headline beat.

daily_trades.pre_market_context: Jackson Hole Symposium begins Aug 21-23. Markets in wait-and-see mode ahead of Powell speech Friday. S&P came off multi-week high; weak prior week. NVDA still at ~$175 building into earnings Aug 27. No major data today.
daily_trades.news_summary: No major catalysts. Pre-Jackson Hole positioning. Dollar marginally stronger, yields slightly elevated on uncertainty. S&P struggling at 6,300 level after prior-week pullback. Semiconductors led lower by NVDA/AMD.

trade_ideas.thesis: Jackson Hole approaching; market widely expects dovish pivot from Powell. Pre-event positioning in long bonds makes sense. Even hawkish surprise has limited downside given labor market softening. Asymmetric setup: bull case +5-7% if Powell confirms cuts; bear case -2-3%.
trade_ideas.catalyst: Powell speech Aug 22 at Jackson Hole
trade_ideas.outcome: WIN at T10. Short-term dip after Jackson Hole (as initial reaction already priced), then accelerated higher into September as jobs data confirmed rate cut certainty. Patience required.

trade_ideas.thesis: Gold benefits from two vectors: (1) lower real yields as Fed pivots dovish, (2) USD weakness on rate cut expectations. Jackson Hole catalyst same as TLT. Gold near multi-year highs but momentum intact. Risk: if Powell disappoints and stays hawkish, GLD -2-3%.
trade_ideas.catalyst: Jackson Hole dovish signal + subsequent weak jobs data
trade_ideas.outcome: STRONG WIN. Gold ripped from $308 to $331 over the period. Best single trade of the period. Dovish Fed + weak jobs = perfect storm for gold.

trade_ideas.thesis: NVDA rallied into earnings (Aug 27 date) but street whisper for data center revenue very high. Second consecutive quarter of data center miss risk. Stock up 35% YTD — bar extremely high. Historically NVDA sells off even on good beats if guidance doesn't dramatically exceed. Short pre-earnings, cover by Aug 28.
trade_ideas.catalyst: NVDA Q2 FY26 earnings Aug 27 AH — data center miss risk
trade_ideas.outcome: LOSS. NVDA rallied from $175 to $182 pre-earnings (pre-event momentum), then dipped to $180 day after. Short was wrong direction pre-earnings — stock climbed into event. The thesis (data center miss) proved correct but the timing was wrong: stock moved against the position before event. Should have waited for earnings to be announced to short the AH pop, not pre-position short.

trade_ideas.thesis: USD dollar should weaken materially if Jackson Hole confirms rate cuts. Dollar index has been elevated on rate differential; closing that differential means USD falls. Short UUP (USD bull ETF) as a bet on dovish Fed and closing US-rest-of-world rate gap.
trade_ideas.catalyst: Jackson Hole dovish signal
trade_ideas.outcome: FLAT/SLIGHT LOSS. USD weakness materialized only partially — UUP moved from 26.55 to 26.43 on Jackson Hole day (correct direction) but then bounced back. Dollar proved more resilient than expected, perhaps because global growth also slowing, limiting relative dollar weakness. Trade idea directionally correct but magnitude disappointing.

trade_ideas.thesis: Rate cut expectations benefit growth/speculative stocks disproportionately. TSLA had underperformed YTD relative to other Mag7 names. Jackson Hole dovish catalyst should lift TSLA as its valuation is highly rate-sensitive. Musk-related sentiment was neutral heading into the week.
trade_ideas.catalyst: Jackson Hole rate cut catalyst — growth stock re-rating
trade_ideas.outcome: WIN. TSLA jumped from $324 to $347 within a week post-Jackson Hole. Rate cut narrative drove the entire Mag7 move; TSLA captured significant beta.

daily_trades.day_pnl_summary: Setup day: 5 trades initiated. No exits. Portfolio skewed long risk-on (TLT + GLD + TSLA) with one short (NVDA pre-earnings, wrong). USD short flat.

daily_trades.pre_market_context: Jackson Hole Symposium formally underway. Powell speech confirmed for tomorrow 10am EDT. Markets generally quiet ahead of speech. NVDA flat at ~$175. S&P 500 at 6,302 (down slightly).
daily_trades.news_summary: Pre-Jackson Hole positioning. Various Fed officials speaking but Powell's Friday remarks are the key. Weak economic data from Europe adding to global easing narrative. No major US data. S&P trading sideways near 6,300.

trade_ideas.thesis: With Jackson Hole tomorrow and strong probability of dovish signal, risk/reward skewed to upside. Market had already pulled back from August highs — entry at 6,300 SPY level with clear catalyst tomorrow. If Powell disappoints (hawkish), S&P likely -1.5% to 6,200; if dovish (base case 75%+), S&P +2% to 6,430+. EV positive.
trade_ideas.catalyst: Jackson Hole Powell speech 8/22 10am EDT
trade_ideas.outcome: WIN. S&P rallied sharply on 8/22 Jackson Hole speech, closed at 639.88 that day (+1.5%). Held through week for +1.49% to 8/29.

trade_ideas.thesis: AMD is derivative of NVDA data center story. If NVDA disappoints on data center (expected), AMD will get hit harder given smaller scale and less visibility into DC revenue. AMD also needs to grow Instinct GPU market share vs NVDA — no evidence of meaningful share gains yet. Short AMD into NVDA earnings week as a 'cleaner' short than NVDA itself.
trade_ideas.catalyst: NVDA earnings overhang + AMD's own data center uncertainty
trade_ideas.outcome: STRONG WIN. AMD dropped from $163 to $151 over the period — down 7.7%. NVDA earnings disappointment (data center miss) dragged AMD further. AMD's own lack of near-term catalyst made it a better short than NVDA which had the underlying business momentum. Thesis held perfectly.

daily_trades.day_pnl_summary: Pre-event positioning day. Added SPY long and AMD short. Portfolio now clearly positioned for Jackson Hole dovish outcome.

daily_trades.pre_market_context: THE key macro event of the period. Powell speech at Jackson Hole at 10am EDT. Markets on edge. Expectation: dovish signals but not fully committing. Some risk of disappointment if Powell stays data-dependent without clearer Sep cut language.
daily_trades.news_summary: Powell delivered a clearly dovish speech at 10am. Key quote (paraphrased): 'The time has come for policy to adjust.' Explicitly flagged downside risks to employment. Removed the 'shortfalls from maximum employment' language — structural dovish shift. Markets ripped: S&P +1.5%, Nasdaq +2%, Dow +600pts. 10Y yields fell. CME FedWatch Sep cut probability: 87%+. Gold hit new high. USD fell. TSLA surged. NVDA up on broader risk-on.

trade_ideas.thesis: TSLA ripped +5% on Jackson Hole day. Adding to existing TSLA long as momentum confirms rate-cut trade thesis. Musk's AI narrative (xAI/Grok) + rate cut tailwind = double catalyst. Near-term target $360-370.
trade_ideas.catalyst: Confirmed rate cut path + TSLA AI narrative
trade_ideas.outcome: WIN. TSLA continued higher from $340 to $350 over next 10 trading days. Momentum trade worked post-Jackson Hole.

trade_ideas.thesis: NVDA up on Jackson Hole broad risk-on, now at $178 heading into earnings Aug 27. This is the better entry for the pre-earnings short: stock now 5 days from earnings at higher price. Data center miss thesis intact. The 'whisper' for Q3 guidance is $56-58B, vs company guidance of $54B +/-2%. Setup: short here, cover AH Aug 27 or Aug 28.
trade_ideas.catalyst: NVDA Q2 FY26 earnings Aug 27 — expected AH selloff on data center guidance miss vs whisper
trade_ideas.outcome: LOSS. Stock continued to grind higher from $178 to $182 into earnings (momentum), then fell to $180 day after. Short still didn't work despite correct thesis — NVDA was carried by broader market and AI sentiment. Lesson: shorting into event with strong macro tailwind (rate cuts = AI capex up = NVDA demand) is fighting two forces. The data center 'miss' was modest and priced in by AH action that reversed.

daily_trades.day_pnl_summary: Jackson Hole day. TLT/GLD/TSLA/SPY longs all working. NVDA short hurting. AMD short also squeezed slightly on broad risk-on (+$4 on AMD). Net portfolio still positive on the day.

daily_trades.pre_market_context: Post-Jackson Hole. Markets digesting Friday's rally. NVDA building into earnings (Wed Aug 27 AH). No major economic data. Rate cut expectations now firmly embedded in market pricing. S&P near all-time high territory.
daily_trades.news_summary: Markets consolidating post-Jackson Hole. Semiconductor index slightly lower as investors await NVDA earnings. AMD slipping (-2.5%) on data center concerns ahead of NVDA earnings. Gold continued grinding higher. Treasury yields staying near multi-month lows. S&P flat-to-slightly-down at 637.

trade_ideas.thesis: Amazon benefits from rate cuts via: (1) AWS cloud capex investment cycle funded cheaper, (2) consumer/retail arm re-rates with lower discount rates, (3) Kuiper satellite broadband expansion. AMZN underperformed META/MSFT YTD — potential catch-up trade. Also benefits if NVDA/AI ecosystem continues even if NVDA itself disappoints (AWS is end customer).
trade_ideas.catalyst: Rate cut re-rating + NVDA ecosystem positive regardless of earnings direction
trade_ideas.outcome: WIN (modest). AMZN went from $228 to $232 over the period. The Sep 4 pop to $235.68 was notable but short-lived. Steady performer, not explosive.

trade_ideas.thesis: SMCI in structural trouble: (1) accounting/legal issues persist — ongoing DOJ investigation into alleged illegal AI server exports, (2) revenue misses despite AI tailwind, (3) cash flow negative, (4) audit uncertainty. Stock was halted briefly in 2024 and remains a credibility problem. Even in AI boom, SMCI consistently underperforms on execution. Any NVDA earnings disappointment will hit SMCI hardest.
trade_ideas.catalyst: NVDA earnings overhang + SMCI-specific governance risk
trade_ideas.outcome: STRONG WIN. SMCI dropped from $44 to $40.41 over 10 days (-8.3%). The accounting/legal overhang + NVDA data center miss narrative compounded. SMCI's inability to generate clean free cash flow and persistent governance concerns made it the right short in the AI infrastructure space.

daily_trades.day_pnl_summary: Consolidation day. Added AMZN long and SMCI short. Building into NVDA earnings.

daily_trades.pre_market_context: One day before NVDA earnings (Aug 27 AH). S&P near 6,390. NVDA at $181.74 — near-term high. Street estimates: Q2 revenue $46.7B (actual matched); Q3 guide consensus ~$53-54B. Whisper higher. Key risk: data center miss for second consecutive quarter.
daily_trades.news_summary: Pre-NVDA earnings positioning. Semiconductor stocks broadly higher on anticipation. S&P rose to 639.73. NVDA up to $181.74. AMD up to $166.62. Markets showing broad appetite for risk. Oil (XLE) climbing. Nothing major on economic calendar.

trade_ideas.thesis: Best entry for NVDA pre-earnings short: stock at near-term high, one day before earnings. Data center revenue has missed whisper numbers two consecutive quarters. Q3 guidance of $54B matches consensus but whisper is $56-58B — risk of guide-down if DC demand from hyperscalers shows any moderation. Short NVDA here, cover at close of Aug 28 (day after earnings).
trade_ideas.catalyst: NVDA Q2 FY26 earnings AH August 27
trade_ideas.outcome: SMALL WIN. NVDA went from $181.74 to $180.14 over 2 days (earnings + 1 day). Stock dropped in AH on earnings night but pared losses next day — typical 'buy the dip' after initial AH selloff. Short entered at the right peak but the magnitude was limited. This was the right entry vs earlier T09-03 and T09-09 attempts.

trade_ideas.thesis: META is the AI company that has demonstrated most disciplined capital allocation relative to AI spend. Rate cuts reduce hurdle rate for AI ROI. META's LLaMA models gaining traction for enterprise. Reels advertising momentum continuing. No binary earnings catalyst near-term. Long META as 'clean' Mag7 with rate cut tailwind.
trade_ideas.catalyst: Rate cut environment + AI monetization momentum
trade_ideas.outcome: FLAT/SLIGHT LOSS. META basically went sideways from $752 to $750 over the period. No major catalyst to move it. The rate cut tailwind was already somewhat priced into META's elevated multiple (~25x forward). Not a bad trade but capital could have been better deployed in GLD.

daily_trades.day_pnl_summary: Day before NVDA earnings. Portfolio positioning set. NVDA short at best entry yet. META long added.

daily_trades.pre_market_context: NVDA earnings day. After close. Markets in anticipation mode all day. S&P flat at 641. NVDA up slightly to $181.57 during day session — muted action as traders wait for AH.
daily_trades.news_summary: NVDA reported after close: Revenue $46.7B (+56% YoY), EPS $1.05 Non-GAAP. Q3 guide: $54B +/-2%. Data center revenue came in slightly short of highest street estimates for second consecutive quarter. Stock fell ~1-2% in AH trading. Quote from CFO commentary: guidance 'reflects continued strong demand' but no dramatic upside surprise.

nvda_earnings_detail.key_issue: Data center revenue missed highest street estimates for 2nd consecutive quarter
nvda_earnings_detail.stock_reaction_AH: Initially -1 to -2% in AH, recovered next day

trade_ideas.thesis: Post-earnings short at AH open. Q3 guide of $54B vs whisper $56-58B is a guide miss in practice. Stock will reprice lower. Second consecutive DC revenue miss signals potential deceleration. Cover within 2-3 days.
trade_ideas.catalyst: NVDA earnings guide miss vs whisper numbers
trade_ideas.outcome: STRONG WIN. NVDA dropped from $181.57 to $174.15 by Aug 29, and then to $170.75 by Sep 2 (+5.97% gain for the short). The AH initial drop held and extended as markets absorbed the implications of slower DC growth narrative. This was the best NVDA short entry of the period — entering post-earnings at the 'rebound from AH low' price.

trade_ideas.thesis: AMD will sell off further as NVDA's data center growth deceleration narrative spills over. AMD has fewer hyperscaler contracts than NVDA and more exposure to enterprise/gaming cycles which are weaker. Double-down on existing AMD short.
trade_ideas.catalyst: NVDA earnings DC miss + AMD own lack of near-term catalysts
trade_ideas.outcome: STRONG WIN. AMD fell from $167 to $151 over 7 trading days (-9.6%). AMD was even weaker than NVDA post-earnings despite having no earnings of its own — pure sector contagion plus AMD's structural position. Best percentage gain on a short trade this period.

daily_trades.day_pnl_summary: NVDA earnings day. Key catalyst event. Post-earnings positioning initiated. AMD short doubled down. Portfolio now has significant short exposure to semis post-NVDA guide.

daily_trades.pre_market_context: Day after NVDA earnings. NVDA recovering from AH lows — typical 'buy the dip on the AH overreaction' dynamic. Opens near $180. S&P slightly higher at 643. Markets overall holding up on rate cut narrative, ignoring semiconductor weakness.
daily_trades.news_summary: NVDA pared AH losses, trading ~$180. AMD also recovering slightly to $168. Market focus shifting back to macro — upcoming jobs data. Gold continues climbing ($315+). TLT holding $84+. S&P at all-time highs above 6,400. Divergence: broad market strong, semis weak.

trade_ideas.thesis: Adding to gold on NVDA earnings confirmation that AI capex cycle may be plateauing. This combined with rate cut certainty and upcoming jobs report (expected weak) makes gold the best risk/reward in the market. Target: $330+.
trade_ideas.catalyst: Rate cut cycle confirmation + labor market weakness + dollar weakness
trade_ideas.outcome: WIN. Gold continued its run from $315 to $331 by Sep 5. The thesis was exactly right — bad jobs report + rate cut certainty = gold surge.

trade_ideas.thesis: Adding to bond position ahead of jobs data. ADP private payrolls (54K vs est 75K) already pointing to soft August jobs report. Rate cut is now 99%+ certain for September. TLT should break to $86-88 range as markets price in Sep cut and possibly accelerated cutting pace.
trade_ideas.catalyst: Weak August jobs report (release Sep 5) + Sep rate cut certainty
trade_ideas.outcome: WIN. TLT rose from $84.38 to $86.00 on Sep 5 jobs report. Further upside to $87.73 by Sep 11. Held the position well.

daily_trades.day_pnl_summary: Post-NVDA stabilization day. Added GLD and TLT. Portfolio positioning: short semis (NVDA+AMD+SMCI), long macro plays (GLD+TLT+TSLA). Broad market exposure (SPY) flat.

daily_trades.pre_market_context: End of August. Week's key events resolved. NVDA sold off more than expected (now at $174, down from $181 earnings). AMD at $162. Market still near all-time highs despite semi weakness. Going into Labor Day weekend.
daily_trades.news_summary: Late-week sell-off in semis. NVDA down to $174.15. AMD down to $162.63. SMCI falling to $41.54. Gold surging to $318. S&P holding 639. Broad market resilient. Pre-Labor Day volume thinning. Jobs report will be released first full day back after holiday (Tue Sep 2 is ADP, Fri Sep 5 is NFP).

trade_ideas.thesis: Covering existing NVDA shorts partially at $174 for profit. But re-shorting a portion: NVDA hasn't found support yet, next leg down toward $165-168 possible if jobs data is weak (labor market weakness → DC customer budget cuts → NVDA guidance miss next quarter). Light short into long weekend.
trade_ideas.catalyst: Continued DC revenue deceleration narrative + jobs report Sep 5
trade_ideas.outcome: WIN. NVDA continued falling to $167 by Sep 5. Total NVDA short journey: $181.74 → $166.99 over 10 days = 7.9% gain for persistent shorts.

trade_ideas.thesis: Energy sector had been quietly rallying with rate cut expectations (capex costs lower) and crude oil recovering. XLE as inflation hedge if rate cuts reignite commodity demand. Also: if labor market weakness triggers fiscal stimulus talk, energy/infrastructure benefits. CONTRARIAN long given semi weakness dominates headlines.
trade_ideas.catalyst: Rate cut + inflation hedge + capex cost reduction for energy companies
trade_ideas.outcome: LOSS. Energy sold off post-Labor Day as weak jobs report dominated (deflation risk > inflation narrative). XLE fell from $44.15 to $42.67 by Sep 5. Thesis was wrong: weak economy = lower oil demand = energy underperforms. Rate cut not sufficient to offset macro weakness for energy.

daily_trades.day_pnl_summary: Pre-holiday Friday. Took profits on some shorts. Re-shorted NVDA. Added energy as contrarian — this was wrong. Going into Labor Day weekend with portfolio: net short semis, long GLD/TLT/TSLA, small long energy.

daily_trades.pre_market_context: First day back from Labor Day. Markets returning to focus on Friday's NFP (Sep 5). ADP private payrolls due this week. NVDA at $170.75 — continuing lower. AMD at $162.32. Gold at $325.59 — ripping. Sep rate cut probability 99%+.
daily_trades.news_summary: Post-holiday markets opened slightly lower. NVDA continued declining ($170.75). Gold surging to $325.59 — extraordinary run. TLT under slight pressure before NFP ($83.15 — slight dip, likely profit-taking). ADP private payrolls: 54K vs est 75K — second data point confirming labor market weakness.

trade_ideas.thesis: Adding more gold. ADP miss confirms NFP will likely be weak. Gold is THE asset to own into the Sep 5 jobs report. Bad jobs = rate cut + safe haven + USD weakness. GLD already up 8% since 8/20 but momentum unstoppable in this regime.
trade_ideas.catalyst: Friday NFP expected catastrophic miss
trade_ideas.outcome: WIN (modest at this entry). Gold continued from $325 to $331. The big move had already happened — this was a late add. Still profitable but chasing momentum at elevated level.

daily_trades.day_pnl_summary: Post-holiday return. Gold thesis confirming. Maintaining semi shorts. TLT slightly under pressure (temporary).

daily_trades.pre_market_context: Two days before NFP. Markets watching labor data closely. S&P at 638. NVDA at $170.59 — still falling. AMD $162.13. Services PMI (ISM) due today — expected 50.9, beat at 52.0.
daily_trades.news_summary: ISM Services PMI beat at 52.0 vs est 50.9 — expansion territory. Markets interpreted as 'economy still functioning despite labor weakness.' S&P rose slightly. Gold held $328. NVDA marginally lower to $170.59. Trade deficit: $78.3B vs est $77.8B — marginal miss, minimal impact.

trade_ideas.thesis: TSLA sold off from $350 peak (post-Jackson Hole) back to $334. Services PMI beat suggests consumer still spending. TSLA Q3 deliveries (due early October) should benefit from rate cuts making auto financing cheaper. Re-buying TSLA dip within the established uptrend.
trade_ideas.catalyst: Rate cut cycle benefits auto financing + TSLA delivery cycle
trade_ideas.outcome: WIN. TSLA bounced from $334 back to $350 by Sep 5. The rate cut thesis kept working. TSLA proved resilient and ended the period at $350 before continuing to $369 by Sep 11.

daily_trades.day_pnl_summary: Data day — services PMI beat. Markets calm before NFP storm. TSLA re-entered on dip.

daily_trades.pre_market_context: One day before August NFP (released Sep 5 at 8:30am EDT). Markets tense. Pre-positioning for jobs data. Nonfarm productivity Q2: 3.3% beat (est 2.8%) — positive for unit labor costs going down. S&P at 643.
daily_trades.news_summary: Nonfarm productivity Q2 beat 3.3% vs 2.8% — helps Fed's inflation case for cuts without overheating. Markets broadly positive: S&P +0.8%, Dow +350pts to 45,621. Nasdaq +1% — semiconductor stocks caught brief bid. Gold at $326. NVDA slight bounce to $171.63. All sectors mixed ahead of tomorrow's NFP.

trade_ideas.thesis: Final pre-NFP add to TLT. ADP showed 54K private payrolls vs 75K estimate. NFP consensus 75K, real expectation (given ADP) is 20-40K. TLT at $84.71 will likely hit $86-88 tomorrow post-NFP. Best risk/reward for last trade before NFP.
trade_ideas.catalyst: August NFP catastrophic miss (expected) — Sep 5 8:30am
trade_ideas.outcome: WIN. TLT surged from $84.71 to $86.00 on Sep 5 (NFP miss day) and continued to $87.73 by Sep 11. This was the cleanest trade setup of the period — data was pointing to a miss, positioning was clear.

trade_ideas.thesis: MSFT has benefited from AI narrative but faces real challenge: Azure growth deceleration visible in last earnings, Copilot monetization slower than expected. If jobs data is weak, enterprise software spending will be scrutinized. MSFT at 35x PE — expensive for decelerating cloud growth. Unlike META which monetizes directly, MSFT is more exposed to enterprise IT budget freezes.
trade_ideas.catalyst: Enterprise IT spending scrutiny if labor market further weakens
trade_ideas.outcome: WIN. MSFT fell from $504.77 to $491.88 on Sep 5. The weak jobs data hit enterprise software names hardest (budget concerns). MSFT has been an underperformer in this period vs broader Mag7.

daily_trades.day_pnl_summary: Pre-NFP final positioning. TLT max position. Short MSFT as enterprise IT softness play. All chips on rate cut theme working tomorrow.

daily_trades.pre_market_context: NFP day. August jobs report at 8:30am EDT. ADP signaled 54K, consensus 75K. Real market expectation: sub-50K. The number that prints will either confirm the rate cut or create uncertainty.
daily_trades.news_summary: AUGUST NFP CATASTROPHE: +22,000 jobs (vs est 75,000). Unemployment rose to 4.3%. Private payrolls negative. This is the weakest NFP in years. CME FedWatch Sep cut probability: 99.3%. Markets rallied on 'bad news = more cuts' logic. S&P to 6,502, Nasdaq +1%, Dow +350. GOLD surged to $331.05 — new ATH. TLT to $86.00. NVDA fell to $166.99 (AI capex risk from weak economy?). TSLA rallied to $350. MSFT down to $491.88.

trade_ideas.thesis: Covering AH position and taking full profits on gold. NFP miss confirms: rate cuts are coming, dollar will weaken, real yields will fall. Exit gold here at $331 — great run from $308 since period started.
trade_ideas.catalyst: NFP miss confirmation — taking profits
trade_ideas.outcome: WIN. Gold from all entries during this period averaged $310-318 range → exit at $331. Total gold trade: massive win. Best call of the entire period.

trade_ideas.thesis: NVDA continued declining to $166.99 on NFP day. Covering all NVDA shorts at $167. The thesis ran its course: earnings disappointment + macro weakness = NVDA re-priced from $181 to $167. Total move: -7.9% from best short entry.
trade_ideas.catalyst: Taking profits on NVDA shorts — thesis fully expressed
trade_ideas.outcome: WIN. Final cover of NVDA short. Best trade sequence: entered at various prices from $174-182, covered at $167. Total NVDA short program generated 5-8% depending on specific entry.

daily_trades.day_pnl_summary: NFP day — ALL thesis plays confirmed. Exiting gold and NVDA shorts at profit. TLT continues higher. AMD short still running. Excellent close to the period.

best_trades.thesis: Dovish Fed + weak labor market = lower real yields = gold ATH
AMD as cleaner short than NVDA — no near-term catalyst + DC miss spillover
Post-earnings guide miss vs whisper — best entry was day OF earnings at AH rebound
Governance + accounting issues + DC miss narrative = persistent underperformer

worst_trades.lesson: Shorting into strong pre-earnings momentum with macro tailwind (rate cuts) fighting two forces simultaneously. The thesis was correct but timing was wrong.
Contrarian energy play in a weak macro environment. Bad economy = lower oil demand > rate cut benefit for energy. Thesis was wrong direction for this macro regime.

key_lessons.title: Bad news = good news regime fully operational
key_lessons.detail: The entire period was characterized by: weak jobs data → higher rate cut probability → stock market goes up. This regime breaks when: (a) inflation re-accelerates, or (b) recession fears overwhelm rate cut optimism. Neither happened Aug 20 - Sep 5.

key_lessons.title: Pre-earnings shorts require post-earnings entry — not pre-event
key_lessons.detail: NVDA thesis (data center miss) was correct. But stock rallied +4% INTO earnings despite the thesis being right. The optimal entry for earnings shorts is: AH on earnings day when initial panic selloff occurs and then stabilizes — NOT 5-7 days before. Pre-earnings shorts fought market momentum and macro tailwind simultaneously.

key_lessons.title: AMD better short than NVDA even when thesis is about NVDA
key_lessons.detail: When NVDA disappointments, AMD sells off MORE (less defensive, smaller moat, fewer hyperscaler commitments). AMD was the better trade: -9.6% vs NVDA -7.9%, with less pre-earnings squeeze risk. Use the derivative, not the underlying, when shorting on a contagion thesis.

key_lessons.title: Gold in dovish pivot regime: add early, hold long
key_lessons.detail: GLD went from $308 to $331 (+7.4%) in 16 trading days. Every piece of data supported the thesis: Jackson Hole dovish → yield falls → weak jobs → USD weakens → gold surges. When macro thesis is this clear, size up and hold — don't trade around it.

key_lessons.title: SMCI = structural short in any AI cycle
key_lessons.detail: SMCI has governance, accounting, and cash flow problems that persist regardless of AI tailwind. It's the weakest link in the AI server chain. Any AI sector disappointment hits SMCI first and hardest. Always available as a short in a dovish/bearish semis environment.

key_lessons.title: Jackson Hole is a one-day event — position BEFORE, exit quickly
key_lessons.detail: The biggest daily move of the period was Aug 22 (+1.5-2% across major indices). If you waited for confirmation, you missed the move. The setup was clear: 75%+ probability of dovish signal, modest downside risk. Jackson Hole requires pre-positioning, not reactive entry.

key_lessons.title: Energy does not benefit from rate cuts in weak macro
key_lessons.detail: The XLE long was a mistake. In a weak economy (labor market collapsing), lower rates do not offset the demand destruction for oil/gas. Rate cut benefit accrues to: tech (lower discount rates), gold (real yields), bonds (duration). NOT to energy where the primary driver is growth/demand.

key_lessons.title: NFP 22K vs est 75K = generational setup for bond longs
key_lessons.detail: When ADP prints 54K (vs est 75K), the probability of NFP catastrophe is high. Position TLT/bonds aggressively before NFP in that scenario. TLT from $84.71 to $87.73 in one week was one of the cleaner risk/reward setups of 2025.

regime_flags.risk_on_or_off: Risk-ON with defensive tilt (gold+bonds outperformed pure equities)
key_inflection_points: 8/27 NVDA earnings: data center growth deceleration signal
9/5 NFP +22K: labor market collapse confirmed Sep rate cut at 99.3%
what_to_watch_next: September 16-17 FOMC: 25bp cut near-certain, watch language for pace of future cuts
